nativeRange.habitat: Generally found growing emersed or submersed in seasonal wetlands, on the muddy beds of dried ponds and lakes, and in slow-moving freshwater systems across the Pantanal and Caribbean. The 'Cuba' and 'Pantanal' verticillate forms are the most commonly traded aquarium morphs.

decorPreferences: midground to background placement as a colour-accent group

summary: A highly prized red/yellow Neotropical stem plant from the Pantanal region; the verticillate 'Cuba' and 'Pantanal' forms are among the most demanding and most coveted reds in the aquascaping hobby.
careNotes: Ludwigia inclinata is a fragile herbaceous stem plant in the family Onagraceae, native to Central and South America with a particular concentration in the Pantanal wetlands of Brazil. The species is most commonly traded as the verticillate forms — Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Cuba' (the cuban form), 'Pantanal' (the most prized Brazilian form), 'Tornado' (a twisted-leaf variant), and 'Curly' — all of which share broadly similar husbandry requirements. This entry covers the species-level baseline; cultivar-specific colour intensity and leaf form vary. Sources generally agree the plant is among the more demanding reds in the hobby: Aquasabi rates difficulty as 'medium' but with the caveat that without high-intensity light and pressurised CO2 the plant becomes wider and greener with markedly reduced coloration, and most aquascaping references treat the verticillate forms as advanced. Temperature is broadly tolerated — Aquasabi cites 18-30°C — but the comfortable range in practice tends to be 22-28°C. pH preference skews soft and acidic; Aquasabi cites a wide 4-7 range, but most aquascapers target 5.5-7.0 with soft water (gH 0-8, kH 0-6) for best colour development. Lighting requirements are high (approximately 0.5-1 W/L of full-spectrum LED, or equivalent PAR), CO2 injection is strongly recommended, and heavy iron plus a complete micronutrient regime are needed to drive the trademark red-to-yellow gradient on the upper leaves. Growth is fast when conditions are right but the plant is intolerant of fluctuating parameters and will melt at the base or drop leaves if dosing or CO2 is unstable. Placement is midground to background as a colour-accent bush. Generally regarded as one of the standout colour-accent stem plants for show tanks, but rarely recommended to beginners.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings. Cut healthy tops from a mature stem and replant the cuttings directly into the substrate; replanting the cut tops rather than the rooted base is recommended because the original base often dies back after topping. Lateral side shoots produced after topping can be trimmed and replanted in turn for mass propagation. Emersed culture is possible and can be useful for stock recovery.

primary.name: Wikipedia
primary.notes: Primary source after Buce Plant product page returned no species-specific content. Used for taxonomy (Onagraceae, L.f.) M.Gomez authority), native range (Central and South America), habitat (emersed/submersed on dried pond/lake beds), morphology (opposite leaf pairs, green-to-red gradient, wavy texture), cultivar identification (var. verticillata 'Pantanal' and 'Cuba'), and general aquarium recommendations (medium-high light, iron and micronutrient dosing, CO2 highly recommended, stem-cutting propagation).

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Secondary source for husbandry parameters on the 'Cuba' cultivar: temperature 18-30°C, pH 4-7, gH 0-30 dGH, kH 0-8 dKH, lighting high (~0.5-1 W/L), CO2 required, growth rate fast, height 15-60 cm, placement middle-to-background, propagation by cuttings (replant cut shoots to avoid die-off), nitrate 5-15 mg/L, phosphate 1-2 mg/L, iron and micronutrient supplementation required, care medium. Used to constrain the parameter ranges; final pH/gH/kH narrowed from Aquasabi's wide tolerance to the soft-acidic sweet-spot commonly cited by aquascapers for best colour.
